extraction.one_line_summary: ALAPHILIPPE won from a select group on the uphill finish at Camaiore after ALMEIDA's 30km attack with 12s gap nearly succeeded but was caught inside final kilometer.
tactical_patterns: BERNAL attacked early signaling Ineos' aggressive new racing style, forcing defensive response from non-climbing sprinters immediately.
ALMEIDA's 30km solo effort with teammate ALAPHILIPPE in chase group is classic Deceuninck quick-step sacrifice-decoy tactic.
VAN DER POEL launched too late (inside 50m) on uphill sprint, revealing poor positioning discipline cost him stage.
ASGREEN and ŠTYBAR provided extended leadout for ALAPHILIPPE through technical climb, showing Deceuninck commits multiple domestiques to medium-mountain finishes.

rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: sustained 30km breakaway with 12s gap nearly successful, showing exceptional diesel engine for future long-range moves
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: went backwards when ALMEIDA pressed on climb, surprisingly weak for defending champion on grippy terrain
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: neutral: strong enough for second but positioning error left sprint launch too late inside 50m
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: perfectly timed sprint using teammate's leadout, clinical execution on uphill finish reveals strong climbing sprint form
rider_signals.SIVAKOV Pavel: neutral: in late break but caught, showing willingness to attack but insufficient power on this terrain
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: defended leader's jersey with 4-second margin, showing strong positioning discipline throughout grippy finale
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: initiated early aggressive move signaling Ineos tactical shift, forcing field to react defensively
